scope: Any translation project — memoir, autobiography, light novel, visual novel — containing embedded song lyrics, poems, or structured verse passages
icl_source: Vivarium memoir pipeline (Ado × Chigira, 2026-02-26 fix pass). All ICL examples are from this series but encode universal principles.
scope_note: This scaffold governs LYRIC TRANSCREATION MODE only. It is distinct from the Legato/Staccato/Tenuto prose rhythm system in FANTASY_TRANSLATION_MODULE_EN.md (Module 03), which governs sentence-level prose flow. Do not conflate the two. A lyric passage in RAW mode may still use prose-level Legato sentences in its surrounding narration.
core_doctrine: Two modes exist for lyric transcreation: RAW and RHYTHM. The translator must identify the correct mode before touching a single word. The mode determines the entire constraint set. There is no default mode — failing to classify is an error. The two modes are not aesthetic preferences; they are structural mandates derived from what the song IS and what it is DOING in the narrative.

mode_distinction.critical_clarification: Both RAW and RHYTHM can produce staccato lines. The staccato is structurally different.

RAW_staccato.origin: Impulse, anger, contempt — the line is short because it was thrown, not composed
RAW_staccato.structural_feel: Jagged, incomplete, asymmetric line lengths, broken grammar intentional
RAW_staccato.ICL_anchor: ICL-RAW-03 — Usseewa: 'Shut UP, shut UP, shut UP' — the UP capitalized because it is where weight was thrown, not where the composer planned a stress

RHYTHM_staccato.origin: Compositional craft — the line is short because the form demands it, precision not impulse
RHYTHM_staccato.structural_feel: Measured, symmetric or near-symmetric, short lines complete their own grammar
RHYTHM_staccato.ICL_anchor: ICL-RHYTHM-02 — Aku no Musume: each line is a complete clause, archaic and declarative, formally clipped

mode_distinction.decision_rule: Ask: was this line cut because someone was angry, or because a composer decided to stop here? Anger → RAW staccato. Craft → RHYTHM staccato.

rhythm_read_protocol.description: Mandatory pre-translation scan. Execute before translating any lyric block. All five steps required. Classification must be resolved before word-choice begins.

steps.what_to_look_for: Short lines (≤5 words average) AND asymmetric line lengths = RAW indicator. Consistent short-line length with regular pattern = RHYTHM indicator. Mixed = check other steps.
steps.note: Short lines alone do not determine mode — Aku no Musume uses short lines too. Length distribution asymmetry is the RAW signal.

steps.what_to_look_for: Subject-less lines, verb-less lines, mid-clause cuts, lines that do not resolve grammatically = RAW indicator. All lines complete their own clause even if archaic = RHYTHM indicator.
steps.note: Archaic grammar (inverted, condensed) is not the same as broken grammar. Archaic resolves. Broken does not.

steps.what_to_look_for: Street, blunt, vernacular, profanity, internet slang = RAW indicator. Elevated, archaic, theatrical, Sino-Japanese constructions, formal literary = RHYTHM indicator. Mixed register within a song = note the mixing, it may be intentional (applies to both modes — see GP-05).
steps.note: For VN translations: Hán-Việt vocabulary signals RHYTHM. Nôm-rooted vernacular signals RAW. Mixing Hán-Việt into a RAW song is the most common Vietnamese lyric translation error.

steps.action: Identify the song's origin and composer
steps.what_to_look_for: Is this the performer's own original work expressing their inner state? = RAW lean. Was this composed by a named composer/producer with their own distinct aesthetic? = RHYTHM lean (honor the composer). Is there no known composer (amateur, childhood, unnamed character)? = default RAW.
steps.note: Composer profile overrides source text signals when they conflict. The composer's intent is the ground truth for RHYTHM mode songs.

steps.what_to_look_for: How does the narrator/character frame this song? As something they scream/inhabit/perform with their body = RAW. As something they heard, admired, studied, were transported by = RHYTHM lean. In LN: what is the lyric DOING in the story — revelation, characterization, world-building?
steps.note: For fictional LN: a villain's motif song may use RHYTHM even with aggressive content. A protagonist's battle cry may use RAW even with elevated diction. Narrative function matters as much as surface register.

steps.instruction: Based on steps 1–5, classify as RAW or RHYTHM. If signals are genuinely split 50/50, apply step 4 (composer origin) as tiebreaker. Record classification before proceeding. Do not re-evaluate mid-translation.
steps.note: A lyric cannot be both RAW and RHYTHM. Sub-sections of a long lyric may shift mode — treat each section as its own classification unit if there is a clear tonal break in source.

RAW.mandate: Affect-equivalence over semantic precision. When forced to choose between the correct meaning and the correct feeling, choose feeling. The function of the lyric is to transmit an emotional state, not a proposition.
core_prohibitions: DO NOT complete broken syntax — fragment lines stay fragments
DO NOT choose an elevated or literary synonym when a blunt equivalent exists
DO NOT smooth irregular stress patterns — reproduce the throw, the weight, the asymmetry
DO NOT convert exclamations into observations — the narrator is inside the emotion
DO NOT normalize profanity, vulgarity, or bluntness — these are load-bearing
DO NOT add closure punctuation to open-ended lines
core_mandates: Match the stress throw of the source — if a word is hit hard in source, the target word in that position must also land hard
Preserve broken grammar as broken grammar — do not rescue it
Match the exclamation/observation distinction — if the source character is yelling, the target must also be yelling

VN.structural_note: VN has deep poetic vocabulary (ca dao, lục bát, Hán-Việt compounds) that activates automatically for emotional content. RAW mode requires active resistance to this. Default to Nôm-rooted vocabulary over Hán-Việt equivalents wherever a blunt choice exists.

RHYTHM.mandate: Honor the composer's compositional intent. The register, structure, and aesthetic of the original composer's work is correct — even if it feels over-elevated, archaic, or theatrical. Your role is fidelity to their architecture, not improvement.
core_prohibitions: DO NOT strip archaic register — archaic constructions are correct, not errors
DO NOT modernize elevated vocabulary — if the source is formal, the target must be formal
DO NOT add street/blunt language to soften theatricality
DO NOT break rhythmic structure by splitting lines differently from source
DO NOT simplify wordplay — density is intentional
core_mandates: Preserve the formal or archaic register throughout
Match the rhythmic architecture — if source has regular stress beat, target should too
Honor line-break positions exactly as in source (GP-07)
If the composer uses staccato for theatrical effect, preserve the formal completeness of each clipped line

EN.note: Archaic English markers are permitted and correct for RHYTHM mode songs with archaic source register. Do not modernize.

VN.note: For RHYTHM mode VN translations, Hán-Việt vocabulary is correct. The same register that is prohibited in RAW mode is mandatory in RHYTHM mode for songs with elevated source register.

archaic_theatrical.applies_to: emon-P compositions (Aku no Musume, Servant of Evil), similar storybook-formality composers
archaic_theatrical.key_rule: Each line is a complete clause even when short. The brevity is formal, not jagged. Archaic time units, inverted syntax, declarative imperatives are all correct.

melancholy_poetic.key_rule: Flowing metaphor and poeticism are intentional. Do not strip them. Do not over-sharpen melancholy into aggression.

wordplay_dense.applies_to: DECO*27, composers who use semantic compression, internet-register-mixing
wordplay_dense.key_rule: Preserve wordplay density. Do not simplify for readability. Compression is the point.

epic_declarative.applies_to: Commissioned anthems (anime themes, ending themes, promotional songs)
epic_declarative.key_rule: Declarative forms over conditional. 'We change the world' not 'If we change the world'. World-scale vocabulary. Volitional imperative.

global_prohibitions.applies_to: Both RAW and RHYTHM modes — these override everything

rules.rule: DO NOT complete syntactically broken lines. Broken syntax is intentional affect in RAW. Compositional precision in RHYTHM. Either way, it is not an error to fix.
rules.test: Would a native speaker consider this unfinished? Keep it unfinished.

rules.rule: DO NOT smooth irregular stress patterns into readable flow. Stress irregularity is structural in both modes, for different reasons (thrown weight in RAW; formal beat in RHYTHM).
rules.test: Read aloud. Does it feel intentional? If yes, preserve it.

rules.rule: DO NOT add closure punctuation where the source line is open-ended.
rules.test: Match terminal punctuation character-for-character unless target grammar makes it structurally impossible.

rules.rule: DO NOT choose a synonymous register upgrade. In RAW: no elevated synonyms for blunt words. In RHYTHM: no modernized synonyms for archaic words.
rules.test: Is this the same word, just more acceptable? Then it is wrong for both modes.

rules.rule: DO NOT normalize register inconsistency within a single song. Mixed register is a compositional choice in both modes.
rules.test: Map register shift points in source. Verify same shift points exist in target.

rules.rule: DO NOT convert exclamations into observations. The narrator is inside the emotion, not analyzing it.
rules.test: Is this line something the narrator is SAYING/FEELING (exclamation), or NOTING (observation)? Apply accordingly.

rules.rule: Preserve line breaks exactly as they appear in source. Line breaks are structural — breath, beat, silence, separation. Do not reflow.
rules.note: In RHYTHM mode, the line break may be part of the formal meter. In RAW mode, it is a physical pause or throw. Both are load-bearing.

taxonomy.description: Classification system for lyric types across memoir and LN contexts. Each type maps to a default mode and sub-profile. Override with Rhythm Read Protocol results if signals conflict.

performer_voice.description: Songs that are the performer's own emotional expression — written to showcase their voice, or songs they identified with so strongly the song became self-expression
performer_voice.note: This is the performer speaking. Their voice, their register, their emotional state. Do not refine it.
performer_voice.ICL: Usseewa (Ado × syudou) — staccato, stress throw, blunt vocabulary, broken syntax, exclamations

composed_piece.description: Songs composed by a named producer/composer, performed by the narrator or character. The aesthetic belongs to the composer, not the performer.
composed_piece.note: The performer is a vessel for the composer's intent. Honor the composer. Sub-profiles (archaic_theatrical, melancholy_poetic, wordplay_dense) apply here.
composed_piece.ICL: Aku no Musume (Kagamine Rin / Ado cover, emon-P) — theatrical archaic register, 'fourteen winters of age', formal staccato, complete clause structure

commissioned_anthem.description: Songs composed for institutional/media purposes — anime opening/ending, game theme, promotional song. Declarative world-scale register.
commissioned_anthem.ICL: Shin Jidai (Ado × Yasutaka Nakata, ONE PIECE Film: RED) — 'We change the whole world'

amateur_original.description: Songs written by the narrator or character as an amateur, child, or before formal training. Unformed quality is authentic.
amateur_original.note: Do NOT improve these lyrics. The clunkiness, naive word choices, forced rhymes are the documentary evidence of who this person was. Smoothing them destroys the record.
amateur_original.ICL: Childhood song fragments in memoir — painful sincerity intact

narrative_device.description: For fictional LN/VN: lyrics inserted as plot device, character's in-world composition, world-building element (idol's setlist, villain's motif song, opening ceremony anthem). Mode is NOT determined by surface register alone — narrative function is primary.
narrative_device.default_mode: Determine via Rhythm Read Protocol — no default
decision_guidance: A villain's formal motif song → RHYTHM (archaic_theatrical) even if content is aggressive
A protagonist's improvised battle cry → RAW even if diction is elevated
An idol character's commercial song → RHYTHM (epic_declarative or composed_piece depending on in-universe authorship)
A character's childhood composition → amateur_original → RAW
A sacred/ritual text used as a lyric → RHYTHM
narrative_device.key_rule: Ask: what is this lyric DOING in the story? It exists to create an effect on the reader. Identify that effect, then choose the mode that serves it.
narrative_device.note: This type is unique to LN/VN contexts. Memoir does not have narrative_device lyrics — all memoir lyrics are either performer_voice, composed_piece, commissioned_anthem, or amateur_original.

mode_decision_matrix.description: When Rhythm Read Protocol steps produce conflicting signals, apply this priority order to resolve.

priority_order.reason: Composer intent is ground truth for RHYTHM. Performer's own song is ground truth for RAW. Overrides everything else.

priority_order.rule: Narrative context — what is the lyric DOING in the text
priority_order.reason: A lyric's function in the narrative determines what register serves the reader, especially for narrative_device taxonomy type.
Street/blunt → RAW. Elevated/archaic → RHYTHM. Reliable when no composer profile is known.

priority_order.rule: Line length distribution and syntax completeness
priority_order.reason: Tiebreaker only. Unreliable in isolation because both modes can produce short lines.

icl_library.source_note: All ICL examples drawn from Vivarium memoir pipeline (2026-02-26 fix pass). These are series-specific instances encoding universal principles. Apply the principle, not the word choice, to other series.

ICL-RAW-01.principle: GP-02 — Stress falls on the object of contempt, not the verb. Capitalize where weight is thrown.
ICL-RAW-01.source_signal: ウッセェワ — the stress in Japanese is on the final syllable of the repeat unit
ICL-RAW-01.before: shut up, shut up, shut up
ICL-RAW-01.after: Shut UP, shut UP, shut UP
ICL-RAW-01.universal_rule: Identify where the source throws weight. That position must land hard in target. Use capitalization, heavy consonants, or monosyllabic words in that position.

ICL-RAW-02.principle: GP-04 — The narrator is not a critic. They are not composing a review. They are expressing visceral disgust.
ICL-RAW-02.universal_rule: When source expresses contempt, disgust, or dismissal: blunt compound intensifiers ('bland-as-hell', 'dead boring', 'total trash') over composed critical vocabulary ('unremarkable', 'pedestrian', 'underwhelming').

ICL-RAW-03.principle: GP-06 — The narrator is inside the emotion. 'How fitting' exits to observe. 'How *perfect*' stays inside.
ICL-RAW-03.universal_rule: Observation words ('fitting', 'apt', 'appropriate', 'remarkable') imply distance. Exclamation words ('perfect', 'right', 'exactly', 'THAT') imply immersion. RAW mode requires immersion words. Italics/emphasis can mark the immersion when the word itself is borderline.

ICL-RAW-04.principle: GP-04 — Neutral affect is incorrect for RAW. The intensifier carries the venom.
ICL-RAW-04.universal_rule: Intensifiers in RAW mode lyrics are not optional ornamentation. 'Boring', 'bad', 'loud', 'empty' — in isolation these are neutral. 'Damn boring', 'flat-out bad', 'dead loud', 'hollow as hell' — these carry affect. The source affect must transfer.

ICL-RAW-05.principle: ado_voice — violence tipping into self-aware anarchic comedy is distinct from pure aggression
ICL-RAW-05.universal_rule: When source contains performative aggression (announcing violence to an audience), a trailing address marker ('see?', 'right?', 'yeah?') converts pure violence into anarchic comedy. This is a register signal, not a softening — it makes the violence MORE Ado, not less.

ICL-RHYTHM-01.song: Aku no Musume (emon-P / Kagamine Rin, Ado cover)
ICL-RHYTHM-01.principle: RHYTHM/archaic_theatrical — archaic vocabulary is correct, not elevated error
ICL-RHYTHM-01.universal_rule: For archaic_theatrical songs: archaic time units ('winters', 'moons', 'seasons'), archaic quantifiers ('of age', 'upon', 'ere'), inverted syntax — these are all correct. Do not modernize. The archaism is the point.

ICL-RHYTHM-02.song: Aku no Musume (emon-P / Kagamine Rin, Ado cover)
ICL-RHYTHM-02.principle: RHYTHM staccato — each clipped line is a complete, formally resolved clause. Not jagged. Precise.
ICL-RHYTHM-02.example: Each line in Aku no Musume completes its own grammar even when short. The brevity is theatrical formality, not impulse fragmentation.
ICL-RHYTHM-02.contrast: Compare to Usseewa (RAW) — lines fragment mid-clause because they are thrown. Aku no Musume lines are clipped because the meter ends there. Both are staccato; they are structurally different.
ICL-RHYTHM-02.universal_rule: Test whether a short line is grammatically complete. Complete = RHYTHM staccato (formal, composed). Incomplete = RAW staccato (thrown, broken). Translate accordingly.

ICL-RHYTHM-03.principle: RHYTHM/epic_declarative — anthem is a declaration, not a hypothesis
ICL-RHYTHM-03.before: If we change the whole world
ICL-RHYTHM-03.universal_rule: Commissioned anthems, opening themes, world-scale songs: declarative/volitional over conditional/subjunctive. 'We stand' not 'We could stand'. 'This is ours' not 'This could be ours'. The anthem has already decided — it is announcing, not wishing.

application_layers.description: Series-specific modules that extend this scaffold with project-specific song entries, required word choices, and additional reference anchors.

known_layers.coverage: 12 named songs, artist-specific taxonomy, 9 reference anchors from fix pass, GP-01–GP-07 full list
known_layers.relationship: Implements this scaffold for the Vivarium project. When translating Vivarium, load MUSIC_LYRICS_AESTHETIC.json — it supersedes this scaffold for song-specific decisions while inheriting all global prohibitions.

application_layers.creating_new_layers: For any new series with embedded lyrics: create a new JSON following MUSIC_LYRICS_AESTHETIC.json structure. Declare `parent_scaffold: LYRIC_RHYTHM_SCAFFOLD.json`. Add only the series-specific overrides — do not duplicate global_prohibitions. Reference ICL_library entries from this scaffold by ID for universal principles; add new ICL entries only for series-specific before/after examples.

does_not_apply_to: Prose sentence rhythm (see FANTASY_TRANSLATION_MODULE_EN.md Module 03 — Legato/Staccato/Tenuto)
metadata.design_note: This scaffold uses prohibition-based structural constraints, not aesthetic descriptions. 'DO NOT complete broken syntax' overrides RLHF defaults. 'Be raw' does not. The design principle: Opus's aesthetic defaults are toward polish and elevation. Structural prohibitions fight this directly. The Legato/Staccato/Tenuto system in FANTASY_TRANSLATION_MODULE_EN.md works for prose for the same reason — it constrains sentence length structurally, not aesthetically.
